topic: Light Waves: Dispersion, Lenses & Image Formation

subUnits.title: 11.1 Dispersion of White Light, Converging & Diverging Lenses, and Image Formation
notes: Dispersion, Lenses, and Image Formation
Key Concepts:
1. Dispersion of White Light:
- Splitting of white light into constituent colors (spectrum) due to different refractive indices for different wavelengths
- Order: Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue, Indigo, Violet (ROYGBIV)
- Demonstrated by triangular prism
2. Lenses:
a) Converging (Convex) Lens: Thicker at center, converges parallel rays to focus
b) Diverging (Concave) Lens: Thinner at center, diverges parallel rays
3. Lens Formula & Sign Convention:
- 1/f = 1/u + 1/v (f = focal length, u = object distance, v = image distance)
- Real-is-positive convention: f positive for convex, negative for concave; u always negative; v positive for real, negative for virtual
4. Magnification:
- m = v/u = image height / object height
- m > 1: magnified; m < 1: diminished; m = 1: same size
5. Image Formation Rules:
- Convex: Object beyond 2F → real, inverted, diminished; between F and 2F → real, inverted, magnified; within F → virtual, upright, magnified
- Concave: Always virtual, upright, diminished
6. Nigerian Context:
- Rainbow formation after Nigerian rainy season
- Magnifying glasses in markets and schools
- Corrective lenses for myopia/hypermetropia in Nigerian optometry
Conclusion:
- Dispersion splits white light into spectrum due to wavelength-dependent refraction
- Convex lenses converge; concave diverge; lens formula predicts image position
- Magnification determines image size
- Mastery supports optics, vision correction, and instrument design

Common misconceptions and how to avoid them

quiz.q: Dispersion of white light occurs because:
opts: All colors travel at same speed
Different colors have different refractive indices
quiz.explanation: Dispersion occurs because different wavelengths refract by different amounts in a medium.

quiz.q: The correct order of colors in a spectrum is:
quiz.explanation: Standard spectrum order: Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue, Indigo, Violet.
Convex lenses are thicker at the center and converge parallel rays to a focus.
Magnification m = v/u = image height / object height.

quiz.q: In Nigerian context, rainbows form due to:
opts: Refraction, dispersion, and internal reflection in raindrops
quiz.explanation: Rainbows result from refraction, dispersion, and total internal reflection in water droplets.

quiz.q: A concave lens always produces an image that is:
quiz.explanation: Concave lenses always produce virtual, upright, diminished images regardless of object position.

quiz.q: A common misconception about lenses is:
opts: Lens formula applies to thin lenses
quiz.explanation: Concave lenses only form virtual images; convex lenses can form real or virtual depending on object position.

quiz.q: If an object is placed at the focal point of a convex lens, the image forms:
quiz.explanation: When object is at focus, refracted rays are parallel, so image forms at infinity.
Myopia (short-sightedness) is corrected with concave lenses to diverge light before it enters the eye.
